ogTitle: Squeaking Point vs St Leonards: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Squeaking Point and St Leonards.
heading: Squeaking Point vs St Leonards — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Squeaking Point, Tasmania and St Leonards, Tasmania. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Squeaking Point has a lower overall climate risk score (34/100) compared to St Leonards (36/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Squeaking Point or St Leonards safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Squeaking Point has a flood risk score of 20/100, while St Leonards scores 53/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Squeaking Point scores 38/100 for bushfire risk. St Leonards scores 18/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Squeaking Point or St Leonards?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Squeaking Point has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.85% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
